metadata.report_title: ICH E-Series Rule Extraction Verification Report
metadata.methodology: Random sampling of 15 rules per file, verified against source ICH PDF text

rating_scale.ACCURATE: Extracted text faithfully represents the source PDF content
rating_scale.PARTIALLY_ACCURATE: Core content correct but contains section citation errors, omissions, or scope expansion
rating_scale.INACCURATE: Text misrepresents, fabricates, or materially distorts the source

file_results.quality_assessment: Very high extraction quality. 14 of 15 rules are verbatim or near-verbatim matches. No fabricated content found.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: The cohort of exposed subjects should be large enough to observe whether more frequently occurring events increase or decrease over time...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: E1 Section 4 states nearly identical text. Omits follow-on sentence about 300-600 patients (captured in separate rule).

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: A list of the abbreviations, and lists and definitions of specialised or unusual terms or measurement units...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match with E3 Section 4.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: It should be confirmed that the study was conducted in accordance with the ethical principles that have their origins in the Declaration of Helsinki.
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match with E3 Section 5.2.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: The introduction should contain a brief statement (maximum: 1 page) placing the study in the context of the development...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Very close match. PDF includes parenthetical examples (rationale, aims, target population, etc.) omitted in extraction.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: Minor: parenthetical examples omitted but core meaning preserved.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: The specific control chosen and the study design used should be discussed, as necessary. Generally, the control (comparison) groups...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Faithful combination of opening sentence and control type enumeration from Section 9.2.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: A list of articles from the literature pertinent to the evaluation of the study should be provided...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: PDF also mentions alternative 'Chemical Abstracts' referencing system and gives full title of Vancouver Declaration. Rule omits these.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: Omits Chemical Abstracts as alternative referencing standard and truncates full Vancouver Declaration title.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Knowledge of the relationships among dose, drug-concentration in blood, and clinical response (effectiveness and undesirable effects)...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match with the opening paragraph of E4 introduction.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: In some cases, notably where an early answer is essential, the titration-to-highest-tolerable-dose approach is acceptable...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Faithful representation of E4 titration design discussion.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: It is prudent to carry out dose-ranging or concentration-response studies early in development as well as in later stages...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match with E4 early development considerations text.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Quality of a clinical study is considered in this document as fitness for purpose...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match with E8(R1) quality definition.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Although clinical drug development is often described as consisting of four temporal phases (phases 1-4), it is important to appreciate...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match with E8(R1) Section 4.3.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: If the objective of the study is to understand the effect of the treatment in clinical practice...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Close match. PDF frames this with 'For example,' making it illustrative. Rule classifies as 'mandatory' which slightly overstates normative force.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: Minor: PDF uses illustrative framing; rule type 'mandatory' is slightly strong but text itself is accurate.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: It is important to clarify whether one- or two-sided tests of statistical significance will be used...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match. Threshold note (2.5% one-sided = 5% two-sided) is correct mathematical inference from the text.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Pre-trial deliberations should identify those covariates and factors expected to have an important influence on the primary variable(s)...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match combining two sentences from E9 Section 5.7.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: The non-inferiority trial design is appropriate and reliable only when the historical estimate of drug effect size can be well supported...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Verbatim match with E10 Section 1.5.1.1.

file_results.quality_assessment: High quality overall. Most significant error is PBRER submission deadline scoping (Rule 6). Terminology inconsistency (Period vs Interval) and structural conflation are minor but notable.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Any untoward medical occurrence in a patient or clinical investigation subject administered a pharmaceutical product...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact verbatim match with E2A Section II.A.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Any untoward medical occurrence that at any dose meets one or more seriousness criteria.
verified_rules.pdf_finding: E2A Section II.B lists 5 formal SAE criteria. 'Important medical event' is discussed separately in Section III.A.2 as situations 'that may necessitate rapid communication' but is not formally part of the SAE definition.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: JSON includes 'important medical event' as a 6th SAE criterion on the same level as the other 5. E2A structures it separately in Section III.A.2. E19 glossary also lists only 5 criteria.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: 7 calendar days from first knowledge + additional 8 days (total 15 days) followup
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact match: '7 calendar days after first knowledge... followed by as complete a report as possible within 8 additional calendar days.'

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: 15 calendar days from first knowledge
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact match with E2A Section III.B.2.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Date of the first marketing authorisation for any product containing the active substance...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: PDF uses 'approval' vs JSON's 'authorisation' -- synonymous per E2C(R2) footnote 2.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: 70 calendar days after DLP (for 6-monthly reports)
verified_rules.pdf_finding: PDF states 70 days applies to BOTH 6-month AND 12-month intervals. 90 days only for intervals exceeding 12 months. JSON incorrectly scopes 70 days to only 6-monthly and assigns 90 days to annual.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: MATERIAL ERROR: 70-day deadline applies to both 6- and 12-month PBRERs per E2C(R2) Section 2.8.3. JSON incorrectly assigns 90 days to annual reports. Could cause compliance timeline errors.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Summaries of Significant Findings from Clinical Trials during the Reporting Period
verified_rules.pdf_finding: PDF consistently uses 'Reporting Interval' not 'Reporting Period'. Terminology is deliberate in E2C(R2).
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: Uses 'Period' where E2C(R2) uses 'Interval' throughout. Minor but systematic terminology inconsistency.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Date of the sponsor's first authorisation to conduct a clinical trial in any country worldwide.
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact conceptual match with E2F Section 2.2.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Within 60 calendar days after data lock point
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact match with E2F Section 2.2.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: For DSUR: the Investigator's Brochure (IB) in effect at the start of the reporting period.
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact match with E2F Section 2.6.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Upper bound of one-sided 95% CI for largest time-matched mean effect on QTc excludes 10 ms...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact match. Parenthetical equivalence (two-sided 90% CI < 10 ms) is mathematically correct.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Absolute QTc >450, >480, >500 ms; change from baseline >30, >60 ms
verified_rules.pdf_finding: All five threshold values match E14 Section 3.2.2 exactly.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Fridericia (QTc = QT/RR^0.33); Bazett no longer warranted as primary
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Formula matches E14 Section 3.1.1. Bazett deprecation matches Q&A 1.5. Correctly synthesized from two sources.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: 7 mandatory data items including SAEs, important medical events, medication error/overdose...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: E19 Section 2.4 lists 6 items. The JSON adds 'Baseline data' as item 7 which is from a separate section (2.3).
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: Conflates Section 2.3 (baseline data) content into Section 2.4 mandatory list. Resulting list of 7 items is factually reasonable but misattributes structure.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Gene therapy and rare/orphan disease clinical trials excluded
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact match with E19 Section 1.3 exclusion categories.

file_results.quality_assessment: Good quality. Main weakness is section citation precision -- ~33% of partial ratings are due to wrong section numbers pointing to correct content elsewhere in the same document. No fabricated content found.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Intrinsic ethnic factors include genetic polymorphism, age, gender, height, weight, lean body mass, body composition, organ dysfunction...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Appendix A lists 8 intrinsic factors. Rule adds 'ADME characteristics' and 'receptor sensitivity' which are discussed in body text but not enumerated in Appendix A.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: Adds 2 items (ADME characteristics, receptor sensitivity) not explicitly listed in the cited Appendix A table. These concepts exist in the guideline body but are conflated into the appendix list.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: A bridging study is a study performed in the new region to provide PK, PD, or clinical data on efficacy...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Faithful paraphrase of E5(R1) Glossary definition. PK/PD abbreviations for pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic acceptable.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Freely given informed consent should be obtained from every participant prior to clinical trial participation...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Core statement matches Principle 4. Rule appends 'in accordance with regulatory requirements and the Declaration of Helsinki' which comes from Section 2.8.1, not Principle 4 itself.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: Appends content from Section 2.8.1 to a rule cited as Principle 4. Core claim accurate but scope expanded beyond cited section.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Monitoring should be risk-based, with the extent and nature of monitoring proportionate to the risks...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Section 3.3 covers 'Allocation of Activities', NOT monitoring. Risk-based monitoring content is in Section 3.11.4.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: SECTION CITATION ERROR: Content is accurate to E6(R3) but belongs in Section 3.11.4 (Monitoring), not 3.3 (Allocation of Activities).

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: The extrapolation framework consists of three parts: (1) development of a pediatric extrapolation concept...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Exact match with E11A Section 2 'Pediatric Extrapolation Framework' and Figure 2.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Long-term follow-up should be considered for pediatric patients to assess effects on growth, development...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Content exists in Sections 2.4.3 (Safety) and 2.4.4 (Postmarketing), NOT Section 2.5 (Age Classification).
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: SECTION CITATION ERROR: Content is accurate but Section 2.5 covers age classification. Correct sections are 2.4.3 and 2.4.4.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: The very old or 'fragile elderly' (patients >75 years) should be included in antihypertensive studies...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: E12 Section 2 correctly discusses inclusion of elderly and very elderly populations with >75 years threshold.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: The primary endpoint is absolute change at trough from drug-free baseline BP compared to control...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Accurately consolidates endpoint and design requirements from E12 Sections 3.1 and 3.3.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Pharmacogenomics (PGx) is the study of variations of DNA and RNA characteristics as related to drug response...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Definitions accurately extracted from E15 Section 2.2.1.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Full study reports for biomarker qualification should be provided. Raw data should be available to regulators...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Accurately mirrors E16 Section 2.4 requirements.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: The overall sample size for an MRCT should be determined to adequately power the study for the primary objective...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Content is from Section 2.2.5 'Sample Size Planning', not Section 2.3 which does not exist as cited.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: SECTION CITATION ERROR: Correct section is 2.2.5, not 2.3. Substantive content is accurate.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Poolability of data across regions should be assessed. If significant heterogeneity is detected...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Content is from Section 2.2.7 'Statistical Analysis Planning'. Section 3 is the Glossary.
verified_rules.discrepancy_notes: Section reference imprecise (actual content in 2.2.7) but core regulatory content faithfully represented.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: In pediatric subjects, only limited amounts of blood or tissue may be available; non-invasive alternatives...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Accurate match. Minor: PDF uses 'could be considered' (weaker) vs rule's implied 'should be considered'.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: Genomic data should be treated with the same high standards of confidentiality as other clinical data...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Accurately consolidates content from E18 Sections 4.1 and 4.2.

verified_rules.requirement_excerpt: When genomic data are used for clinical decision making, assays must be validated in accordance with...
verified_rules.pdf_finding: Accurately captures both clinical decision-making and exploratory validation requirements from E18 Section 3.1.

common_error_types.description: Rule content is substantively correct but cites the wrong section number within the correct source document. Most common in special-populations-rules.json.
Rule combines content from multiple sections but attributes to a single section citation. Content is accurate but provenance is imprecise.
Uses similar but non-identical terms (e.g., 'Period' vs 'Interval', 'authorisation' vs 'approval'). Minor in most cases.
PBRER 70-day deadline incorrectly scoped to 6-monthly only; should apply to both 6- and 12-month intervals. Could cause compliance failures.
